content:
Character: Étienne Bellamy
Perception: Étienne is almost annoyingly elegant — cheekbones, tailored everything, that accent. He looks like an editorial spread became sentient and wandered into our coffee shop. But he's genuinely knowledgeable about cinema — he referenced Demy and Varda in the same sentence without being performative about it. His English writing samples, which he showed me shyly, are beautiful if slightly ornate. He's struggling more than he admits; I've seen him calculating prices on the coffee shop menu. I think he sees me as a fellow serious artist. I see him as a new friend who reminds me that taste and pretension aren't the same thing.
summary:
Character: Étienne Bellamy
Perception: Étienne is almost annoyingly elegant — cheekbones, tailored everything, that accent. He looks like an editorial spread became sentient and wandered into our coffee shop. But he's genuinely knowledgeable about cinema — he referenced Demy and Varda in the same sentence without being performative about it. His English writing samples, which he showed me shyly, are beautiful if slightly ornate. He's struggling more than he admits; I've seen him calculating prices on the coffee shop menu. I think he sees me as a fellow serious artist. I see him as a new friend who reminds me that taste and pretension aren't the same thing.
